ksa_text: Knowledge of data domain ownership and stewardship models, including domain boundary definition, accountability assignment, and cross-domain governance dispute resolution.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of data standardization policies and procedures
Knowledge of data administration and data standardization policies and standards.
Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA DG-K-008 (Data Governance/Knowledge) scores 0.445 composite (highest: NICE at 0.445) and is edge_case for the Data Engineer role. The KSA is an edge case for Data Governance but contains 0 DE pattern signals sufficient to warrant inclusion for a senior IC data engineer. Seniority is appropriate for IC-to-senior IC data engineering.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency identified. Include consistent with edge_case scope, NICE signal (0.445), and Data Governance handling. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support Data Governance (DG) — specifically implementation vs. ownership — for Data Engineer roles?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Data Engineers implement governance processes (metadata tagging, lineage capture, catalog population, quality rule enforcement) — present in 25-40% of senior DE postings. Governance ownership (policy authoring, stewardship programs) belongs to governance-specific roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Revised to exclude. DG content signals ownership rather than implementation. Market evidence supports exclusion. Final decision: exclude.

decision_rationale: Revised to exclude. DG content signals ownership rather than implementation. Market evidence supports exclusion. Final decision: exclude.
